# Quick Fix: Sequential Permission Requests to Prevent CoreBluetooth XPC Crash
Generated: 2026-02-18T14:25:19Z

## Change Made
- File: `attendance_app/lib/features/permission/data/datasources/permission_local_datasource.dart`
- Line(s): 68-89
- Change: Replaced batch `toRequest.request()` call with a sequential for-loop that calls `def.permission.request()` one at a time, wrapping each in try/catch to handle individual failures gracefully.

## Verification
- Syntax check: PASS (manual review - valid Dart syntax)
- Pattern followed: Existing PermissionItem construction loop preserved as-is

## Files Modified
1. `attendance_app/lib/features/permission/data/datasources/permission_local_datasource.dart` - requestAll() now requests permissions sequentially

## Notes
- The root cause of CoreBluetooth XPC crashes is simultaneous permission dialogs; sequential requests ensure each dialog completes before the next is shown.
- Individual catch blocks treat any exception as PermissionStatus.denied, keeping behavior safe under failure.
